req.title: Search patients
req.statement: A clinician shall search patients by name, MRN, or DOB; only patients in their care shall be returned.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a clinician can search patients via /Patient?search using name, MRN, or DOB, with results scoped strictly to their own care panel, access logged on every call, and a 400 returned for malformed queries. I derived: (1) a positive EP test for a valid name search returning only in-care patients; (2) a negative NEG test confirming 400 on a malformed query string; (3) a boundary BVA test using a single-character name query (minimum meaningful input); (4) a DT test exercising the access-control rule — a patient NOT in the clinician's care panel must be absent from results; (5) an STC test confirming the access log entry is created after a successful search. This covers all three mandated kinds (positive, negative, boundary) and exceeds the minimum with richer coverage.

tests.name: test_search_by_name_returns_in_care_patients_only
preconditions: At least one patient named 'Smith' is in the clinician's care panel (seeded via <seed_in_care_patient>)
At least one patient named 'Smith' is NOT in the clinician's care panel (seeded via <seed_out_of_care_patient>)
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param name=Smith using clinician auth headers
Assert HTTP 200
Assert response is a FHIR Bundle
Assert every returned Patient resource belongs to clinician's care panel
Assert out-of-care patient MRN is absent from the bundle
expected: All entries are patients in clinician's care panel
Out-of-care patient is not present in results
trace: R-HLT-013: only patients in their care shall be returned
tests.executable:
def test_search_by_name_returns_in_care_patients_only(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    seed_in_care_patient,
    seed_out_of_care_patient,
    api_client,
):
    in_care_mrn = seed_in_care_patient["mrn"]
    out_of_care_mrn = seed_out_of_care_patient["mrn"]

    resp = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "name=Smith"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"

    returned_mrns = [
        entry["resource"]["identifier"][0]["value"]
        for entry in bundle.get("entry", [])
    ]

    assert in_care_mrn in returned_mrns, "In-care patient should appear"
    assert out_of_care_mrn not in returned_mrns, "Out-of-care patient must not appear"

tests.name: test_malformed_query_returns_400
actions: GET /Patient?search with a malformed query string (e.g. search=%%%INVALID)
Assert HTTP 400
Assert response body contains an OperationOutcome or error detail
expected: Response contains error descriptor (OperationOutcome or equivalent)
trace: R-HLT-013: error pathways — 400 when query malformed
tests.executable:
def test_malformed_query_returns_400(clinician_auth_headers, api_client):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "%%%INVALID"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    # Accept either FHIR OperationOutcome or a plain error key
    has_error = (
        body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
        or "error" in body
        or "issue" in body
    )
    assert has_error, f"Expected error descriptor in body, got: {body}"

tests.name: test_single_char_name_query_is_valid_boundary
preconditions: At least one in-care patient whose name starts with 'A' exists (seeded via <seed_in_care_patient_a>)
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param name=A (single character — minimum meaningful input)
Assert HTTP 200 (single char is accepted)
Assert response is a FHIR Bundle
Assert all returned entries belong to clinician's care panel
expected: Bundle with zero or more entries, all in clinician's care panel
No server error (5xx) or client rejection (400) for a one-character name
tests.executable:
def test_single_char_name_query_is_valid_boundary(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    seed_in_care_patient_a,
    api_client,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "name=A"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Single-char name query must be accepted, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"

    in_care_mrn = seed_in_care_patient_a["mrn"]
    returned_mrns = [
        entry["resource"]["identifier"][0]["value"]
        for entry in bundle.get("entry", [])
    ]
    assert in_care_mrn in returned_mrns, "Seeded in-care patient starting with A must appear"

tests.name: test_out_of_care_patient_never_returned_regardless_of_search_param
preconditions: A patient with MRN known to be outside the clinician's care panel exists (seeded via <seed_out_of_care_patient>)
The out-of-care patient's name, MRN, and DOB are known
actions: GET /Patient?search with name matching the out-of-care patient
Assert HTTP 200
Assert out-of-care patient MRN is absent from the bundle
Repeat with MRN search param
Repeat with DOB search param
expected: Out-of-care patient MRN absent in all three bundles
Access control enforced regardless of which search dimension is used
trace: R-HLT-013: only patients in their care shall be returned
R-HLT-013: search by name, MRN, or DOB
DT: all three search parameters tested against care-panel access-control rule
tests.executable:
def test_out_of_care_patient_never_returned_regardless_of_search_param(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    seed_out_of_care_patient,
    api_client,
):
    p = seed_out_of_care_patient  # keys: mrn, name, dob
    out_mrn = p["mrn"]

    def returned_mrns(resp):
        bundle = resp.json()
        assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
        return [
            e["resource"]["identifier"][0]["value"]
            for e in bundle.get("entry", [])
        ]

    for param in [
        {"search": f"name={p['name']}"},
        {"search": f"mrn={out_mrn}"},
        {"search": f"dob={p['dob']}"},
    ]:
        resp = api_client.get("/Patient", params=param, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
        assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 for {param}"
        assert out_mrn not in returned_mrns(resp), (
            f"Out-of-care patient must not appear when searching by {param}"
        )

tests.name: test_successful_search_creates_access_log_entry
preconditions: At least one in-care patient exists (seeded via <seed_in_care_patient>)
Access log can be queried via <audit_log_client> using the clinician's subject ID
actions: Record timestamp before search
GET /Patient?search with name=Smith using clinician auth headers
Assert HTTP 200
Query audit log for an entry matching clinician ID, action=search, resource=Patient, after recorded timestamp
Assert exactly one log entry exists for this search event
expected: Audit log contains an entry: actor=clinician, action=Patient.search, recorded after request timestamp
trace: STC: system transitions from pre-search state to post-search state with audit record created
tests.executable:
def test_successful_search_creates_access_log_entry(
    clinician_auth_headers,
    seed_in_care_patient,
    api_client,
    audit_log_client,
    clinician_subject_id,
):
    import time
    before_ts = int(time.time())

    resp = api_client.get(
        "/Patient",
        params={"search": "name=Smith"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    log_entries = audit_log_client.query(
        subject=clinician_subject_id,
        action="Patient.search",
        after=before_ts,
    )

    assert len(log_entries) >= 1, "At least one audit log entry must be created"
    entry = log_entries[0]
    assert entry["subject"] == clinician_subject_id
    assert entry["action"] == "Patient.search"
    assert entry["timestamp"] >= before_ts
